config.nameExtra: Letters, digits, underscore, dash and dot only; up to 64 characters.
config.nameInvalid: Only letters, digits, underscore, dash and dot are allowed
config.nameInvalidLong: Invalid name: only letters, digits, underscore, dash and dot are allowed.
config.defaultTokenExtra: The token must match between server and client. Leave empty for now and fill it in later.

editor.basicHintServer: Where the server listens and the token used to verify clients.
editor.basicHintClient: The server to connect to and the shared token.
editor.defaultTokenPlaceholder: Leave empty to require per-service tokens
editor.advancedHeaderClient: Advanced: heartbeat / retry / transport (TLS · Noise · WebSocket)
editor.heartbeatIntervalExtra: Server-side heartbeat interval. Default 30, 0 to disable.
editor.heartbeatTimeoutExtra: Connection is considered down after this many seconds without a heartbeat. Default 40, 0 to disable.
editor.transportHintServer: The transport protocol and credentials used between launcher and peers.
editor.transportHintClient: Must match the transport configured on the server.
editor.deleteConfirmContent: This cannot be undone. Stop the configuration first if it is running.

services.hint: A configuration can expose / forward several services at once (SSH, web, database…).
services.empty: No services yet. Click "Add service" to create the first one.
services.tokenExtra: Leave empty to use the global default_token.

transport.protocolHint: TCP by default. TLS / Noise add encryption; WebSocket is useful when only HTTP(s) is allowed.
transport.hostnameExtra: Empty falls back to the host portion of remote_addr for SNI.

log.title: Live log
log.empty: Real-time rathole logs will appear here once started.

welcome.serverLead: Run on a machine with a public IP to expose internal services.
welcome.clientLead: Run on the internal machine to register local services with the public server.
welcome.warningDetail: Place {bin} next to {dir}, or override the path under {settings}.
welcome.serverGuideDesc: Runs on the public-IP machine, listens for reverse connections from inside clients and forwards external requests to the matching local service.
welcome.serverGuideAttr1: address that clients dial in to
welcome.clientGuideDesc: Runs on the internal machine, dials out to the server and registers local services (SSH, HTTP, database…) so external traffic can reach them via the server.
welcome.emptyHintServer: Click + on the left to create your first server configuration.
welcome.emptyHintClient: Click + on the left to create your first client configuration.

settings.title: Settings
settings.lead: The launcher uses these paths when starting rathole. Configurations are stored in the server_conf and client_conf folders inside {dir}.
settings.binaryHint: Drop the rathole binary next to the launcher to auto-detect, or override the path here.
settings.dirsHint: Click to open in the system file manager (path is also copied to clipboard).
settings.externalsSection: Other rathole processes on this machine
settings.externalsHint: The launcher never touches processes it didn't start. It only warns when ports collide.
